svg.aria-label: Two acts. First, megabrain_search: no-LLM retrieval ranks the chunks and the LLM rerank strikes the vocabulary-only matches. Then megabrain_ask: a broad question fans out into three parallel sub-agents, one synthesis merges their cited answers with the verbatim code spliced in, and the workflow lands in the flow cache.

g.text: $ megabrain search . "how does the rerank drop noise"
19 signal chunks (31 pruned) · 2 ms · no LLM
rerank: one cheap LLM pass · ~1–2 s · fail-open
→ your agent reads signal, not a haystack
the rerank can only drop, never invent — retrieval stays deterministic
next ▸ what happens when the question is BROAD
$ megabrain ask . "how does indexing work end to end?"
classifier reads the bundle shape — BROAD → fan out · no LLM
Code splits over its syntax tree —
retrieval tools only · no file writes
Every chunk embeds in ONE batch
call — pplx by default, or any local
retrieval tools only · no file writes
Chunks + vectors land in one SQLite
file — incremental by sha, no vector
retrieval tools only · no file writes
synthesis — one model merges the agents' answers, citing their [[k]] spans
the model points · the engine splices
Indexing chunks code over its AST [[0]], embeds it in one batch [[3]], and stores vectors in SQLite [[5]] —
def index_repo(root: Path, force: bool = False) -> dict:
changed = [f for f in walk(root) if sha_changed(f)] # incremental
indexer.py L40 · verbatim, true line numbers
⚡ the whole workflow lands in the flow cache — the next related ask retrieves it at once, a near-exact repeat serves with zero LLM
sub-agents carry retrieval tools only — every citation resolves to real disk bytes before it renders, so nothing here can be invented
